other_observers: John Vanderpoel, Beth Dillon and Joanie Kleypas.; Bird originally found by Dave Leatherman and Joe Mammoser.
specific_location: Grandview Cemetary in central Fort Collins
description: Smallish warbler, but with a quite long, flesh colored bill. The head, neck, wing coverts and upper tail were a dark blue. Two white wingbars were very conspicuous and easy to see. There was a small olive patch in the center of the back that was much more difficult to see. The area of the cheeks was blackish, below the dark eye and behind the bill. There was absolutely no sign of eye arcs that I could see. The chin, breast and belly were quite bright yellow, with the upper breast being slightly darker and a little more orangish. The under tail coverts were white, and the legs were dark.Behaviors: The bird was singing and foraging mostly near the tops of several deciduous trees in a surburban cemetary.Call: The bird was singing almost continuously, but I couldn't distinguish it from that of Northern Parula.Plumage: Alternate or Breeding
